  against future glob behavior change; Bun.Glob currently non-recursive)

Test summary: 668 pass / 0 fail (was 632 in main; added 36 new tests).
EOF

content.thinking:
Now I'm mapping out P3, which brings together the directory watching system from path B, the workflow attachment lifecycle that handles journal and agent integration, the CLI flag for workflow mode, and the dispatcher that orchestrates everything—this is going to be the most intricate piece, so I need to be deliberate about what's actually in scope. Looking at the spec more carefully, I need to implement WorkflowAttachment.start() which chains journal watcher initialization, scans for existing agents, and launches the subagent directory watch—with snapshot watching deferred to P4. For attachAgent(), I'm setting up individual transcript watchers with proper source labels and guards against duplicate agent IDs. The stop() method tears down all watchers and marks sessions complete. WorkflowDetector handles directory watching for new workflow files in the session's workflows folder, while the main line parsing path stays in P5 scope.

For P3 acceptance, the critical path is the `--workflow <runId>` dispatcher which directly invokes WorkflowAttachment.start(), so WorkflowDetector's background detection can be lightly implemented without heavy test coverage. I'm focusing on the four acceptance criteria: showing journal plus agent transcripts, streaming new journal lines within a second, attaching new agent files, and respecting the `--no-workflow-agents` flag. The implementation needs paths.ts utilities already in place, the WorkflowDetector and WorkflowAttachment classes with their respective start/stop methods, and CLI flag validation for `--workflow`, `--no-workflow-agents`, and `--workflow-pane`.

content.thinking:
Now I'm mapping out the P3 phase, which is the most substantial one yet—I need to focus on three key files: the WorkflowDetector class, the WorkflowAttachment helpers, and CLI parser updates for the new workflow flags with validation logic.

For the dispatcher in index.ts, when the --workflow flag is detected, I'll route to startClaudeWorkflowMultiWatch. To meet the acceptance criteria for T4 through T7, the minimum viable implementation requires WorkflowAttachment with a journal FileWatcher that can scan and attach existing agent files plus monitor the subagent directory, and the dispatcher needs to construct WorkflowAttachment from a runId. The WorkflowDetector's directory watch on workflows/ will be useful for resumed workflows but isn't directly tested by those acceptance criteria, so I'll implement it with a lighter touch and add tests for it.

Review the implementation plan at `/Users/pc035860/code/agent-tail/specs/auto-implement/plan-2026-05-30_18-24-50_workflow-p3.md`.

Context: P3 of a 7-phase workflow-support feature for agent-tail. SPEC: `/Users/pc035860/code/agent-tail/specs/workflow-support/SPEC.md` (focus §9.1 path B, §9.3, §10.2 WorkflowAttachment, §10.4 dispatcher, §11.1 CLI flags, §11.2 mutual exclusions). Previous plans (P1, P2) are at `/Users/pc035860/code/agent-tail/specs/auto-implement/plan-2026-05-30_17-40-18_workflow-p1.md` and `plan-2026-05-30_18-03-05_workflow-p2.md` for context.

P3 scope:
1. NEW `src/claude-workflow/workflow-detector.ts` — path B (directory watch on `{session}/workflows/`)
2. NEW `src/claude-workflow/watch-builder.ts` — WorkflowAttachment class (journal FileWatcher + agent attach + dir watch)
3. MODIFY `src/cli/parser.ts` — add `--workflow [runId]`, `--no-workflow-agents`, `--no-workflow-attach`, `--workflow-pane` + mutual exclusion validation
4. MODIFY `src/index.ts` — dispatcher route for `--workflow`, new `startClaudeWorkflowMultiWatch`

Out of P3 (deferred): SnapshotWatcher integration (P4), path A main-JSONL detection (P5), interactive (P6), pane execution (P7).

Acceptance: T4, T5, T6, T7.

Existing code reference:
- `src/index.ts` has `startClaudeMultiWatch` (line 394) — pattern to mirror
- `src/claude/watch-builder.ts` has `createOnLineHandler` — workflow doesn't reuse this since it has different label semantics
- `src/core/file-watcher.ts:WatchOptions` is the existing FileWatcher contract
- `src/core/detector-interfaces.ts` defines OutputHandler / SessionHandler
- P1's `JournalLineParser` already implements `LineParser`
- P2's `WorkflowSessionFinder.findBySessionId` + `findLatest` give us snapshot path lookup

Focus your review on:
1. **Lifecycle ordering** — `start()` does waitForTranscriptDir → journal start → initial agent scan → dir watch. Should snapshot watcher (P4) eventually wedge BEFORE or AFTER step 3? The P1/P2 plans said "snapshot last" — verify P3 leaves room.
2. **`prefillKnown` API** — P3 uses it (when path A in P5 lands) but isn't called from P3 dispatcher (D8). Is that the right framing? Or should P3 dispatcher pre-register the runId in `WorkflowDetector` even though it doesn't actually start the detector?
3. **`attachAgent` race-safety** — synchronous `knownAgentIds.has` check + add (race-safe per JS event loop), then async file watcher creation. Rollback on failure removes from set. Per SPEC §9.3 / S7.
4. **CLI flag wiring** — Commander's `--no-X` behavior with positive form: are the option names and defaults correct? `withWorkflowAgents` / `workflowAttach` default `true`; `workflowPane` default `false`.
5. **Dispatcher in src/index.ts** — `options.workflow !== undefined` is the dispatch trigger. Is that adequate, or do we need to also handle the case where `findBySessionId` returns a workflow `SessionFile` (customTitle starts with `wf:`) via the existing session-id arg path?
6. **Path A (P5) leakage check** — verify that the P3 plan doesn't accidentally try to wire path A. WorkflowDetector public API has `handleMainLine(parsed)` only in P5.
7. **Tests** — adequate to drive impl + lock contracts? Specifically the integration test could be a flake risk — is it OK to leave it as optional?
8. **`_handleAgentLine` drain loop** — uses local guard=100 like existing parsers. Consider whether to use the `drainParser` helper from `src/utils/parser-drain.ts` instead.

Verdict (PASS / PASS_WITH_CHANGES / BLOCK) with specific patches if any.

1	import { Command } from 'commander';
2	import type { AgentType, CliOptions } from '../core/types.ts';
3	
4	function createProgram(): Command {
5	  const program = new Command();
6	
7	  program
8	    .name('agent-tail')
9	    .description(
10	      'Tail agent session logs (Codex, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Cursor & Antigravity CLI) in real-time'
11	    )
12	    .version('0.1.0')
13	    .argument(
14	      '<agent-type>',
15	      'Agent type: codex, claude, gemini, cursor, or agy'
16	    )
17	    .argument(
18	      '[session-id]',
19	      'Optional session ID to load (partial match supported)'
20	    )
21	    .option('--raw', 'Output raw JSONL instead of formatted output', false)
22	    .option('-p, --project <pattern>', 'Filter by project name (fuzzy match)')
23	    .option('-f, --follow', 'Follow file changes (default: true)', true)
24	    .option('--no-follow', 'Do not follow, only output existing content')
25	    .option('-v, --verbose', 'Show full content without truncation')
26	    .option('--no-verbose', 'Show truncated content (default)')
27	    .option('-q, --quiet', 'Suppress non-error output messages')
28	    .option('--no-quiet', 'Show informational messages (default)')
29	    .option(
30	      '--subagent [id]',
31	      'Claude/Codex/Cursor: tail subagent log (latest if no ID)'
32	    )
33	    .option(
34	      '-s, --sleep-interval <ms>',
35	      'Set file polling interval in milliseconds (default: 500)',
36	      parseInt
37	    )
38	    .option(
39	      '-n, --lines <number>',
40	      'Number of initial lines to show per file (default: all)',
41	      parseInt
42	    )
43	    .option(
44	      '-i, --interactive',
45	      'Claude/Codex/Cursor: interactive mode for switching between sessions (Tab to switch)'
46	    )
47	    .option('--no-interactive', 'Disable interactive mode (default)')
48	    .option(
49	      '--with-subagents',
50	      'Claude/Codex/Cursor: include subagent content in output'
51	    )
52	    .option('--no-with-subagents', 'Exclude subagent content (default)')
53	    .option(
54	      '--auto-switch',
55	      'Auto-switch to latest session in project (Claude/Gemini/Codex/Cursor)'
56	    )
57	    .option('--no-auto-switch', 'Disable auto-switch (default)')
58	    .option(
59	      '-a, --all',
60	      'Claude/Codex/Cursor: show all content (verbose + with-subagents + auto-switch)',
61	      false
62	    )
63	    .option(
64	      '--pane',
65	      'Claude/Codex/Cursor: auto-open tmux pane for each new subagent'
66	    )
67	    .option('--no-pane', 'Disable pane auto-open (default)')
68	    .option('-l, --list', 'List recent sessions instead of tailing')
69	    .option('--summary', 'Show session summary (first lines + last lines)');
70	
71	  return program;
72	}
73	
74	/**
75	 * 解析 CLI 參數
76	 */
77	export function parseArgs(args: string[]): CliOptions {
78	  const program = createProgram();
79	  program.parse(args);
80	
81	  const agentTypeArg = program.args[0];
82	  const sessionIdArg = program.args[1];
83	  const opts = program.opts();
84	
85	  // 驗證 agent 類型
86	  if (
87	    agentTypeArg !== 'codex' &&
88	    agentTypeArg !== 'claude' &&
89	    agentTypeArg !== 'gemini' &&
90	    agentTypeArg !== 'cursor' &&
91	    agentTypeArg !== 'agy'
92	  ) {
93	    console.error(
94	      `Error: Invalid agent type "${agentTypeArg}". Use "codex", "claude", "gemini", "cursor", or "agy".`
95	    );
96	    process.exit(1);
97	  }
98	
99	  // --summary 模式（自動 no-follow，互斥同 --list）
100	  const finalSummary = opts.summary ?? false;

<output-file>/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-pc035860-code-agent-tail/a4125c98-2d39-4722-ae76-ed157651ee39/tasks/aab81778705515b1d.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Review P3 plan" completed</summary>
<result>I have enough context. Here is my review.

---

## Verdict

**PASS_WITH_CHANGES**

The plan is structurally sound, correctly scoped to P3, mirrors the existing `startClaudeMultiWatch` pattern, and respects the phase boundaries set by P1/P2. SPEC §9.1/§9.3/§10.2/§10.4/§11.1/§11.2 are accurately reflected. However, several concrete issues need fixing before implementation begins. Most are mechanical (wrong import paths, non-existent helper, Commander defaults), but two are substantive (lifecycle ordering vs. P4, `Bun.file` exists check, dispatcher-vs-arg routing).

---

## Critical issues (must fix)

### C1 — `createConsoleOutputHandler` does not exist
Step 4.2 (line 542 of plan) writes `const outputHandler: OutputHandler = createConsoleOutputHandler(options);` and the closing note says "reuse it." It does not exist anywhere in the codebase. The real construction is `new ConsoleOutputHandler()` (class, no `options` argument). See `src/claude/output-handlers.ts:11` and 6 callsites of `new ConsoleOutputHandler()` in `src/index.ts`. **Fix**: replace with `new ConsoleOutputHandler()` and import it from `./claude/output-handlers.ts`.

### C2 — `OutputHandler` import path inconsistent with existing code
Plan imports `OutputHandler` from `'../core/detector-interfaces.ts'` (which is correct — that's the canonical location). But existing code in `src/claude/output-handlers.ts:2` imports it from `'./subagent-detector.ts'` (which re-exports). Not a blocker for new files, but the plan should note: **new** files import from `core/detector-interfaces.ts` directly (modern path), not the legacy re-export. Already implicitly correct in the plan — just keep it that way.

### C3 — `_waitForTranscriptDir` uses `Bun.file(dir).exists()` antipattern (then immediately abandons it)
Plan code (line 286-289):
```ts
const dir = Bun.file(this.config.workflow.transcriptDir);
// Bun.file().exists() returns false for dirs; use stat instead.
const { stat } = await import('node:fs/promises');
await stat(this.config.workflow.transcriptDir);
```
The `Bun.file(dir)` line is dead code with no effect. Remove the first line entirely; only the `stat()` call is needed. This is exactly the gotcha listed in CLAUDE.md ("`Bun.file(dir).exists()` returns false for directories. Use `stat(dir)` instead.").

### C4 — Commander `--no-X` + positive `--with-X` flag collision risk
The plan registers BOTH:
```ts
.option('--with-workflow-agents', '...', true)
.option('--no-workflow-agents', '...')
```
Commander uses the **camelCase of the rightmost word after `--`** as the option key. `--with-workflow-agents` → `withWorkflowAgents`. `--no-workflow-agents` → flips `workflowAgents` (different key!). This is the same trap the existing code avoids by using `--with-subagents` + `--no-with-subagents` (matching keys, see parser.ts:52). **Fix**: use `--with-workflow-agents` + `--no-with-workflow-agents` (matching key `withWorkflowAgents`). Adjust SPEC §11.1 wording or update plan to match SPEC exactly: SPEC line 1106 says `--no-workflow-agents` is "（Commander 自動覆寫上一個鍵）" — but Commander **does not** auto-bridge `withWorkflowAgents` and `workflowAgents`. Same problem for `--no-workflow-attach` (the plan only registers the `--no-*` form, no positive form). That's actually fine for Commander (negation-only flag → default true, becomes false when set). But the result mapping line `workflowAttach: opts.workflowAttach !== false` is wrong if no positive `--workflow-attach` was registered — Commander stores `true` by default for negation-only options (so `opts.workflowAttach === true` by default; `=== false` only when `--no-workflow-attach` passed). The `!== false` check works, but I'd rewrite as `opts.workflowAttach ?? true` for clarity.

### C5 — Result-mapping for `workflowAgents` won't match key
Plan line 466 says `withWorkflowAgents: opts.workflowAgents !== false`. But if `--with-workflow-agents` is registered, the key is `withWorkflowAgents` (not `workflowAgents`). The current code in `parser.ts` will read `undefined` from `opts.workflowAgents` → result is `true`. Looks "right" by accident but is fragile. **Fix**: pick one canonical positive form and a matching negative form, then mirror exactly. Recommended pair: `--with-workflow-agents` (positive) + `--no-with-workflow-agents` (negation) → both map to `withWorkflowAgents`, and `opts.withWorkflowAgents !== false` works.

### C6 — Dispatcher gating: `findBySessionId` returning a workflow `SessionFile` not handled
Q5 from the review: yes, this is a real gap. P2 spec'd `ClaudeSessionFinder.findBySessionId('wf_xxx')` to return a workflow `SessionFile` with `customTitle.startsWith('wf:')`. SPEC §11.3 dispatcher line 1076 explicitly checks both:
```ts
const wantsWorkflowMode = options.workflow !== undefined ||
  (sessionFile?.customTitle?.startsWith('wf:') === true);
```
P3 plan only checks `options.workflow !== undefined`. So `agent-tail claude wf_6f7d9da9` (positional arg, no `--workflow` flag) will fall through to `startClaudeMultiWatch` and try to tail a `wf_*.json` as if it were `*.jsonl` — broken. **Fix**: add the second clause to the dispatcher trigger; when triggered via positional path, derive `runId` from `sessionFile.path` (basename) and `snapshotPath = sessionFile.path` (skip the wfFinder call).

### C7 — `prefillKnown` is dead in P3 (D8 framing is half-right)
The plan exports `prefillKnown` but says (D8) "No `prefillKnown` call in P3 dispatcher". That's correct because P3 dispatcher doesn't start the detector at all. But then the prefill API has no caller in P3 and no test. Options:
- (a) Drop `prefillKnown` from P3 entirely (lands in P5).
- (b) Keep it (low cost), but add a unit test verifying behavior so P5 inherits a tested contract (the test row "prefillKnown suppresses dir-watch emission" is in the plan — keep it; that justifies landing the API now).

**Recommended**: keep `prefillKnown` and the test; remove the dead `// Used by dispatchers...` comment that implies a current caller. Rephrase the docblock to "Used by P5 dispatcher (main-JSONL path A)…".

### C8 — Lifecycle ordering: leaves no clear "snapshot wedge slot" for P4
The question is "should snapshot watcher wedge BEFORE or AFTER step 3?" SPEC §10.2 R3-B4 is explicit: snapshot watcher is **Step 4**, after directory watch starts. The P3 plan's `start()` comment block says `// Step 4 — snapshot watcher: P4 scope (not wired here)` — that's correct placement. **However**, the plan does not preserve the SPEC's critical ordering invariant: `journalParser.markLiveMode()` must run **after** initial flush completes, **before** any future state-mutating callback. The current plan calls `markLiveMode()` immediately after `await watcher.start(journalPath, ...)`. That's fine for P3 (FileWatcher's `start()` awaits the initial read, see `file-watcher.ts:49`). But P4 needs to know that adding SnapshotWatcher AFTER markLiveMode is safe. **Recommendation**: add a comment in `_startJournalAndFlushHistory` documenting: "FileWatcher.start() awaits initial readAndProcess (see core/file-watcher.ts:49) so markLiveMode is safe to call synchronously after." This preserves the wedge slot for P4 without ambiguity.

### C9 — Use `drainParser` helper, don't reimplement
Q8: yes, swap `_handleAgentLine`'s hand-rolled guard=100 loop for `drainParser` from `src/utils/parser-drain.ts`. Existing codebase has 5 callsites already using it. Ad-hoc 100-iteration loops are exactly what that helper exists to replace. CLAUDE.md explicitly lists `drainParser` as the canonical pattern. **Note**: `DRAIN_GUARD_MAX` in the helper is 10000, not 100 — the helper's value is the production one. Use it.

### C10 — `--workflow-attach` default mismatch

## Risks & mitigations

- **Risk**: `fs.watch` on `transcriptDir` fires before `agent-*.jsonl` is fully written → `attachAgent` reads empty file. **Mitigation**: `FileWatcher` retries on empty content via its internal polling (existing). But the plan's `attachAgent` doesn't retry on `watcher.start()` failure. The rollback path is there, but on failure no auto-retry happens — relying on the next fs.watch event. For P3 this is acceptable (matches Cursor's `tryAddSubagentFile` rollback pattern), but document this in the plan: "rollback only — retry depends on next fs.watch event."

- **Risk**: Commander's `--no-X` semantics surprise. **Mitigation**: Patch 3a forces matching keys.

- **Risk**: macOS `fs.watch` may not fire for files in a transcriptDir created milliseconds after dir watch starts (race). **Mitigation**: SPEC §19.6 acknowledges this is a known issue. P3 plan correctly does `_scanAndAttachInitialAgents` BEFORE `_startSubagentDirWatch`, which catches files already on disk before the watch races in.

- **Risk**: P4 SnapshotWatcher integration might want to insert BEFORE step 3 (dir watch) if snapshot completion needs to suppress new agent attaches. **Mitigation**: SPEC §10.2 R3-B4 is explicit — snapshot is Step 4. P3 plan leaves correct slot. No action needed in P3; just document the wedge point.

- **Risk**: `process.once('SIGINT')` in `startClaudeWorkflowMultiWatch` swallows SIGINT for the rest of the process. Other code paths register `SIGINT` handlers too (check `src/index.ts:1437+`). **Mitigation**: Use `process.on('SIGINT', ...)` and add `await attachment.stop` to the existing shutdown flow if one exists, or scope the handler with cleanup.

## Verdict

**PASS_WITH_CHANGES**

The plan is structurally sound, correctly scoped to P3, mirrors the existing `startClaudeMultiWatch` pattern, and respects the phase boundaries set by P1/P2. SPEC §9.1/§9.3/§10.2/§10.4/§11.1/§11.2 are accurately reflected. However, several concrete issues need fixing before implementation begins. Most are mechanical (wrong import paths, non-existent helper, Commander defaults), but two are substantive (lifecycle ordering vs. P4, `Bun.file` exists check, dispatcher-vs-arg routing).

---

## Critical issues (must fix)

### C1 — `createConsoleOutputHandler` does not exist
Step 4.2 (line 542 of plan) writes `const outputHandler: OutputHandler = createConsoleOutputHandler(options);` and the closing note says "reuse it." It does not exist anywhere in the codebase. The real construction is `new ConsoleOutputHandler()` (class, no `options` argument). See `src/claude/output-handlers.ts:11` and 6 callsites of `new ConsoleOutputHandler()` in `src/index.ts`. **Fix**: replace with `new ConsoleOutputHandler()` and import it from `./claude/output-handlers.ts`.

### C2 — `OutputHandler` import path inconsistent with existing code
Plan imports `OutputHandler` from `'../core/detector-interfaces.ts'` (which is correct — that's the canonical location). But existing code in `src/claude/output-handlers.ts:2` imports it from `'./subagent-detector.ts'` (which re-exports). Not a blocker for new files, but the plan should note: **new** files import from `core/detector-interfaces.ts` directly (modern path), not the legacy re-export. Already implicitly correct in the plan — just keep it that way.

### C3 — `_waitForTranscriptDir` uses `Bun.file(dir).exists()` antipattern (then immediately abandons it)
Plan code (line 286-289):
```ts
const dir = Bun.file(this.config.workflow.transcriptDir);
// Bun.file().exists() returns false for dirs; use stat instead.
const { stat } = await import('node:fs/promises');
await stat(this.config.workflow.transcriptDir);
```
The `Bun.file(dir)` line is dead code with no effect. Remove the first line entirely; only the `stat()` call is needed. This is exactly the gotcha listed in CLAUDE.md ("`Bun.file(dir).exists()` returns false for directories. Use `stat(dir)` instead.").

### C4 — Commander `--no-X` + positive `--with-X` flag collision risk
The plan registers BOTH:
```ts
.option('--with-workflow-agents', '...', true)
.option('--no-workflow-agents', '...')
```
Commander uses the **camelCase of the rightmost word after `--`** as the option key. `--with-workflow-agents` → `withWorkflowAgents`. `--no-workflow-agents` → flips `workflowAgents` (different key!). This is the same trap the existing code avoids by using `--with-subagents` + `--no-with-subagents` (matching keys, see parser.ts:52). **Fix**: use `--with-workflow-agents` + `--no-with-workflow-agents` (matching key `withWorkflowAgents`). Adjust SPEC §11.1 wording or update plan to match SPEC exactly: SPEC line 1106 says `--no-workflow-agents` is "（Commander 自動覆寫上一個鍵）" — but Commander **does not** auto-bridge `withWorkflowAgents` and `workflowAgents`. Same problem for `--no-workflow-attach` (the plan only registers the `--no-*` form, no positive form). That's actually fine for Commander (negation-only flag → default true, becomes false when set). But the result mapping line `workflowAttach: opts.workflowAttach !== false` is wrong if no positive `--workflow-attach` was registered — Commander stores `true` by default for negation-only options (so `opts.workflowAttach === true` by default; `=== false` only when `--no-workflow-attach` passed). The `!== false` check works, but I'd rewrite as `opts.workflowAttach ?? true` for clarity.

### C5 — Result-mapping for `workflowAgents` won't match key
Plan line 466 says `withWorkflowAgents: opts.workflowAgents !== false`. But if `--with-workflow-agents` is registered, the key is `withWorkflowAgents` (not `workflowAgents`). The current code in `parser.ts` will read `undefined` from `opts.workflowAgents` → result is `true`. Looks "right" by accident but is fragile. **Fix**: pick one canonical positive form and a matching negative form, then mirror exactly. Recommended pair: `--with-workflow-agents` (positive) + `--no-with-workflow-agents` (negation) → both map to `withWorkflowAgents`, and `opts.withWorkflowAgents !== false` works.

### C6 — Dispatcher gating: `findBySessionId` returning a workflow `SessionFile` not handled
Q5 from the review: yes, this is a real gap. P2 spec'd `ClaudeSessionFinder.findBySessionId('wf_xxx')` to return a workflow `SessionFile` with `customTitle.startsWith('wf:')`. SPEC §11.3 dispatcher line 1076 explicitly checks both:
```ts
const wantsWorkflowMode = options.workflow !== undefined ||
  (sessionFile?.customTitle?.startsWith('wf:') === true);
```
P3 plan only checks `options.workflow !== undefined`. So `agent-tail claude wf_6f7d9da9` (positional arg, no `--workflow` flag) will fall through to `startClaudeMultiWatch` and try to tail a `wf_*.json` as if it were `*.jsonl` — broken. **Fix**: add the second clause to the dispatcher trigger; when triggered via positional path, derive `runId` from `sessionFile.path` (basename) and `snapshotPath = sessionFile.path` (skip the wfFinder call).

### C7 — `prefillKnown` is dead in P3 (D8 framing is half-right)
The plan exports `prefillKnown` but says (D8) "No `prefillKnown` call in P3 dispatcher". That's correct because P3 dispatcher doesn't start the detector at all. But then the prefill API has no caller in P3 and no test. Options:
- (a) Drop `prefillKnown` from P3 entirely (lands in P5).
- (b) Keep it (low cost), but add a unit test verifying behavior so P5 inherits a tested contract (the test row "prefillKnown suppresses dir-watch emission" is in the plan — keep it; that justifies landing the API now).

**Recommended**: keep `prefillKnown` and the test; remove the dead `// Used by dispatchers...` comment that implies a current caller. Rephrase the docblock to "Used by P5 dispatcher (main-JSONL path A)…".

### C8 — Lifecycle ordering: leaves no clear "snapshot wedge slot" for P4
The question is "should snapshot watcher wedge BEFORE or AFTER step 3?" SPEC §10.2 R3-B4 is explicit: snapshot watcher is **Step 4**, after directory watch starts. The P3 plan's `start()` comment block says `// Step 4 — snapshot watcher: P4 scope (not wired here)` — that's correct placement. **However**, the plan does not preserve the SPEC's critical ordering invariant: `journalParser.markLiveMode()` must run **after** initial flush completes, **before** any future state-mutating callback. The current plan calls `markLiveMode()` immediately after `await watcher.start(journalPath, ...)`. That's fine for P3 (FileWatcher's `start()` awaits the initial read, see `file-watcher.ts:49`). But P4 needs to know that adding SnapshotWatcher AFTER markLiveMode is safe. **Recommendation**: add a comment in `_startJournalAndFlushHistory` documenting: "FileWatcher.start() awaits initial readAndProcess (see core/file-watcher.ts:49) so markLiveMode is safe to call synchronously after." This preserves the wedge slot for P4 without ambiguity.

### C9 — Use `drainParser` helper, don't reimplement
Q8: yes, swap `_handleAgentLine`'s hand-rolled guard=100 loop for `drainParser` from `src/utils/parser-drain.ts`. Existing codebase has 5 callsites already using it. Ad-hoc 100-iteration loops are exactly what that helper exists to replace. CLAUDE.md explicitly lists `drainParser` as the canonical pattern. **Note**: `DRAIN_GUARD_MAX` in the helper is 10000, not 100 — the helper's value is the production one. Use it.

### C10 — `--workflow-attach` default mismatch
SPEC §11.1 says `--no-workflow-attach` toggles `workflowAttach: true` default. The plan registers `--workflow-attach` (positive form, default `true`) and `--no-workflow-attach` (negation). But P3 dispatcher path never reads `workflowAttach` (it's a P5 main-session concern). So registering it in P3 is correct (locks flag set early), but the result-mapping `workflowAttach: opts.workflowAttach !== false` should be `opts.workflowAttach ?? true` for clarity and to match Commander's actual default storage semantics for negation-only flags. (Cosmetic but worth fixing now.)

- **Risk**: macOS `fs.watch` may not fire for files in a transcriptDir created milliseconds after dir watch starts (race). **Mitigation**: SPEC §19.6 acknowledges this is a known issue. P3 plan correctly does `_scanAndAttachInitialAgents` BEFORE `_startSubagentDirWatch`, which catches files already on disk before the watch races in.

- **Risk**: P4 SnapshotWatcher integration might want to insert BEFORE step 3 (dir watch) if snapshot completion needs to suppress new agent attaches. **Mitigation**: SPEC §10.2 R3-B4 is explicit — snapshot is Step 4. P3 plan leaves correct slot. No action needed in P3; just document the wedge point.

- **Risk**: `process.once('SIGINT')` in `startClaudeWorkflowMultiWatch` swallows SIGINT for the rest of the process. Other code paths register `SIGINT` handlers too (check `src/index.ts:1437+`). **Mitigation**: Use `process.on('SIGINT', ...)` and add `await attachment.stop` to the existing shutdown flow if one exists, or scope the handler with cleanup.

---

## Test plan

Unit:
- `WorkflowDetector` (per plan rows) — all 7 rows; add: "outputHandler.debug called when dir doesn't exist" (mock debug fn, assert call).
- `WorkflowAttachment` (per plan rows) — all 8 rows; add: "attachAgent rollback clears agentWatchers + agentParsers + knownAgentIds" (regression for Patch 2d).
- Parser — all plan rows + Patch 5a/5b additions.

Integration:
- `tests/integration/workflow-multi-watch.test.ts` — **promote from optional to required**. Minimum coverage: fixture writes journal + 2 agent jsonls + dispatch → assert output buffer contains 3 distinct labels and a journal line.

Manual smoke (after P3 lands, on dev box):
```bash
agent-tail claude --workflow wf_<existing>     # T4
echo '{"type":"started",...}' >> .../journal.jsonl  # T5
touch .../agent-{17hex}.jsonl                  # T6
agent-tail claude --workflow wf_<x> --no-with-workflow-agents  # T7
```

---

## Open questions

None — the review's 8 focus points are all answerable from spec/code as described above.

lines: -> Commander note: `--no-workflow-agents` flips the value of the option whose **canonical name** is `workflowAgents`. Use `.option('--no-workflow-agents', ...)` to register the negation. The positive form `--with-workflow-agents` registers `workflowAgents` (the lookup key Commander uses to map both flags).

+> Commander note: matching key pair `--with-workflow-agents` (positive, registers `withWorkflowAgents`) + `--no-with-workflow-agents` (negation, flips same key) avoids the trap where mismatched keys silently no-op the negation flag.

<result>All ten patches landed cleanly. The plan now reads correctly end-to-end. A few residual nits surfaced on re-read — none blocking, calling them out below for awareness only.

## Verdict

**PASS** (with minor residual nits — non-blocking)

## Residual issues

### R1 — D8 trade-off entry contradicts updated `prefillKnown` docblock (cosmetic)
The `prefillKnown` docblock (lines 80-86) now correctly says "P3 does not call this — included now so the API contract is tested before P5 wires it." Good. But Decision **D8** in the trade-offs table (line 604) still reads:

> No `prefillKnown` call in P3 dispatcher | Direct attach via `--workflow <runId>` skips the detector entirely — detector is started ONLY when running normal mode (P5 path A). P3 dispatcher path does not start detector.

The rationale is accurate, but the framing reads like the API has zero P3 footprint, which now contradicts the "included now so the API contract is tested" docblock. **Suggested wording**: "P3 dispatcher path does not start the detector; `prefillKnown` ships in P3 only to lock the API contract via tests before P5 wires it."

### R2 — Line 14 in Overview is slightly stale
Line 14 says:
> CLI flags in `src/cli/parser.ts`: `--workflow [runId]`, `--no-with-workflow-agents`, `--no-workflow-attach`, `--workflow-pane` (defined; pane impl P7).

That's an incomplete list — Step 3 actually registers six flag bindings (positive `--with-workflow-agents`, `--workflow-attach`, `--workflow-pane` plus their three negations). The Overview describes only the most "user-facing" forms, which is fine, but a reader cross-checking flag count will see a mismatch. **Suggested fix**: change to "`--workflow [runId]`, `--with-workflow-agents`/`--no-with-workflow-agents`, `--workflow-attach`/`--no-workflow-attach`, `--workflow-pane`/`--no-workflow-pane`" or just note "(plus matching `--no-*` negations)".

### R3 — Line 15 in Overview not updated for C6 (positional-arg path)
Line 15 still says:
> Dispatcher in `src/index.ts`: when `options.workflow !== undefined`, route Claude to `startClaudeWorkflowMultiWatch`.

But Step 4 (and C6's fix) now also routes on `sessionFile?.customTitle?.startsWith('wf:')`. **Suggested fix**: append "...or when the resolved `sessionFile.customTitle` starts with `wf:` (positional `wf_*` arg)."

### R4 — "Critical Files for Implementation" undercount
Line 665 says "MODIFY; 4 new flags + validation". With matching `--no-*` pairs it's now six bindings (or four logical flags + their negations). Either count is defensible; the line is slightly stale relative to Step 3. **Suggested fix**: "MODIFY; new workflow flags + validation" (drop the count).

### R5 — Acceptance Mapping T7 wording
Line 675 references `--no-with-workflow-agents` correctly. Good — this matches the renamed flag. No action.

### R6 — `process.once('SIGINT')` interaction with other handlers (deferred risk, already flagged)
Step 4.2 still uses `process.once('SIGINT', ...)`. The risk I called out earlier (other SIGINT handlers in the file at index.ts:1437+) hasn't been addressed. This is acceptable for P3 since `startClaudeWorkflowMultiWatch` is the only handler installed before the workflow attachment, but worth documenting as a known limitation that P6 (interactive) will revisit. **Suggested**: add a one-liner comment: `// TODO(P6): coordinate SIGINT with interactive shutdown path`.

## What's good

- All ten patches applied verbatim or near-verbatim — implementation can proceed.
- C6 dispatcher fix is correctly two-branch (flag vs positional), and the positional branch correctly reuses the `sessionFile.path` from `ClaudeSessionFinder.findBySessionId` (no duplicate finder call).
- C8's comment in `_startJournalAndFlushHistory` is well-placed and explicit about the wedge slot for P4.
- C9's `drainParser` swap is clean — note the comment correctly cites the 10000-iteration guard (matching the real helper, not the stale `100` from my original critique).
- C4/C5's `--with-workflow-agents` / `--no-with-workflow-agents` pair now matches the existing `--with-subagents` / `--no-with-subagents` convention. Future maintainers won't trip on it.
- Test row 5d (positional-arg) correctly tests the parser layer (`sessionId: 'wf_xxx'`); dispatcher routing is then covered by the integration test promoted in 5d.
- Integration test promoted to required + the explicit recommendation to skip `startClaudeWorkflowMultiWatch` and drive `WorkflowAttachment` directly avoids `process.exit` trap in tests.

## Ready to implement

The plan can be executed as written. The four residual nits above are cosmetic (R1, R2, R3, R4) or deferred (R6) — none block P3 implementation or test contracts. R3 is the most worth fixing before implementation starts since it affects how a reviewer understands the dispatcher trigger.

## Files referenced

- `/Users/pc035860/code/agent-tail/specs/auto-implement/plan-2026-05-30_18-24-50_workflow-p3.md` (under review)
- `/Users/pc035860/code/agent-tail/src/utils/parser-drain.ts` (`DRAIN_GUARD_MAX = 10000` — matches plan's "10000-iteration guard" comment)
- `/Users/pc035860/code/agent-tail/src/cli/parser.ts:49-52` (existing `--with-subagents` / `--no-with-subagents` precedent — plan now matches)
- `/Users/pc035860/code/agent-tail/src/claude/output-handlers.ts:11` (`ConsoleOutputHandler` class — plan now imports correctly)</result>

content.subject: Setup feature branch: feat/workflow-support
content.description: git fetch then git checkout -b feat/workflow-support. Abort if branch exists.

content.subject: [P1] Draft implementation plan
content.description: Phase 1: Paths helpers + types (SessionListItem/ParsedLine/CliOptions extensions, WorkflowSnapshot, JournalEvent, DetectedWorkflow, WorkflowDetectorConfig) + JournalLineParser + SnapshotWatcher. Draft a step-by-step plan covering file structure, types definitions, and unit test fixtures. Reference SPEC sections §3, §6, §8.1, §8.2.

content.subject: [P1] Review plan
content.description: Run plan-reviewer agent on the P1 plan to catch gaps, contradictions, missing acceptance criteria mapping.

content.subject: [P1] Write failing tests (RED)
content.description: TDD RED step. Write failing tests for: paths helpers (getWorkflowsDir, getWorkflowRunDir, cwdToClaudeProjectFilter), JournalLineParser (started/result events, invalid JSON tolerance, markLiveMode behavior), SnapshotWatcher (initial read + debounce + onChange + dedup via lastJson). Tests should fail because implementation doesn't exist yet.

content.subject: [P1] Implement to pass tests (GREEN)
content.description: Create src/claude-workflow/{paths.ts,types.ts,journal-parser.ts,snapshot-watcher.ts}. Modify src/core/types.ts to add SessionListItem workflow fields + ParsedLine workflow event fields + CliOptions workflow flags. Implement until P1 tests pass.

content.subject: [P1] Verify and commit
content.description: Run bun test (P1 scope) + bun run typecheck + bun run lint. Fix issues. Then /commit with conventional commit message describing P1 scope.

content.subject: [P2] Draft implementation plan
content.description: Phase 2: WorkflowSessionFinder (first-class source) + ClaudeSessionFinder integration (export class, add getProjectInfo + findLatestInProject + listSessions internal merge) + SubagentDetector workflows/ exclusion. Plan covers all SessionFinder methods (findLatest/findBySessionId/getProjectInfo/findLatestInProject/listSessions) and list merge logic. Reference SPEC §7, §9.4.

content.subject: [P2] Review plan
content.description: Run plan-reviewer agent on P2 plan. Check SessionFinder interface compliance, listSessions merge ordering, fuzzy filter alignment.

content.subject: [P2] Write failing tests (RED)
content.description: Tests for: WorkflowSessionFinder.findLatest/findBySessionId/listSessions (fixture: ~/.claude/projects/{enc}/{UUID}/workflows/wf_*.json), ClaudeSessionFinder.listSessions merging main + workflow, SubagentDetector skipping workflows/ subdirectory (T17 mixed-subagents fixture). Also test cwdToClaudeProjectFilter encoding. Acceptance targets: T1, T2, T2b, T3, T3b, T17.

content.subject: [P2] Implement to pass tests (GREEN)
content.description: Create src/agents/claude/workflow-agent.ts (WorkflowSessionFinder). Modify src/agents/claude/claude-agent.ts: export ClaudeSessionFinder, add getProjectInfo, findLatestInProject, internal _workflowFinder integration in listSessions + findBySessionId. Modify src/claude/subagent-detector.ts to skip workflows/ subdirectory. Run tests until green.

content.subject: [P2] Verify and commit
content.description: Run bun test + typecheck + lint. /commit with conventional message.

content.subject: [P3] Draft implementation plan
content.description: Phase 3: WorkflowDetector path B (directory watch on workflows/ with markRunIdKnown synchronous guard + rollback) + WorkflowAttachment lifecycle (journal FileWatcher + initial agent attach + subagent dir watch) + --workflow CLI flag + startClaudeWorkflowMultiWatch dispatcher. Reference SPEC §9.1 path B, §9.1.1, §9.3, §10.2, §10.4, §11.1.

content.subject: [P3] Review plan
content.description: plan-reviewer: check lifecycle ordering (history dump before snapshot stop trigger), synchronous markRunIdKnown race-safety, rollback path, --workflow + agentType validation.

content.subject: [P3] Write failing tests (RED)
content.description: Tests for: WorkflowDetector path B (synthetic workflow file creation triggers onNewWorkflow exactly once; rollback on tryHandleNewWorkflow failure), WorkflowAttachment.start step ordering (journal history dump before subagent watch start), attachAgent dedup + rollback. Acceptance T4 T5 T6 T7.

content.subject: [P3] Implement to pass tests (GREEN)
content.description: Create src/claude-workflow/{workflow-detector.ts,watch-builder.ts}. Add WorkflowAttachment class (in watch-builder.ts or separate file). Modify src/cli/parser.ts: add --workflow / --with-workflow-agents / --no-workflow-agents / --no-workflow-attach. Modify src/index.ts: add startClaudeWorkflowMultiWatch dispatcher branch.

content.subject: [P3] Verify and commit
content.description: Run bun test + typecheck + lint. /commit.

content.subject: [P4] Draft implementation plan
content.description: Phase 4: SnapshotWatcher integration into WorkflowAttachment + status events output + fault tolerance (invalid JSON in journal, snapshot read failure, directory removal triggering stop). Reference SPEC §10.2 snapshot stream, §19.2/§19.4 edge cases, acceptance T8 T18 T19 T20.

content.subject: [P4] Review plan
content.description: plan-reviewer: snapshot debounce timing, completed/failed → stop microtask ordering, ENOENT on directory removal triggers stop without main program exit.

content.subject: [P4] Write failing tests (RED)
content.description: Tests for: snapshot update emits status event (T8), invalid JSON in journal line is skipped not crashed (T18), snapshot read failure logs warning and recovers on next update (T19), directory removal triggers WorkflowAttachment.stop('directory-removed') without affecting other attachments (T20). Use temp dir fixtures.

content.subject: [P4] Implement to pass tests (GREEN)
content.description: Wire SnapshotWatcher into WorkflowAttachment (step 4 of start() per SPEC). Add status event output for non-interactive mode. Handle ENOENT in WorkflowDetector + WorkflowAttachment gracefully.

content.subject: [P4] Verify and commit
content.description: bun test + typecheck + lint. /commit.

content.subject: [P5] Draft implementation plan
content.description: Phase 5: ClaudeLineParser detection of Workflow tool_use + async_launched tool_result (populate ParsedLine.isWorkflowToolUse / workflowAsyncLaunch) + WorkflowDetector.handleMainLine path A + OnLineHandlerConfig.workflowDetector integration in createOnLineHandler + --no-workflow-attach flag. Reference SPEC §6.2, §9.1 path A, §9.2, acceptance T9, T9b, T10, T10b, T16.

content.subject: [P5] Review plan
content.description: plan-reviewer: tool_use detection regex precision (only Workflow tool, not Workflow-prefixed names), async_launched status discrimination, no duplicate attach when path A + path B both fire (T16).

content.subject: [P5] Write failing tests (RED)
content.description: Tests for: ClaudeLineParser detects Workflow tool_use + async_launched tool_result and populates ParsedLine fields, WorkflowDetector.handleMainLine triggers onNewWorkflow exactly once per runId, no duplicate when path A + path B race (T16), --no-workflow-attach flag suppresses detection.

content.subject: [P5] Implement to pass tests (GREEN)
content.description: Modify ClaudeLineParser to populate isWorkflowToolUse + workflowAsyncLaunch. Implement WorkflowDetector.handleMainLine. Modify OnLineHandlerConfig to accept workflowDetector. Modify createOnLineHandler to dispatch parsed.workflowAsyncLaunch to detector. Wire detector into startClaudeMultiWatch when workflowAttach !== false.

content.subject: [P5] Verify and commit
content.description: bun test + typecheck + lint. /commit.

content.subject: [P6] Draft implementation plan
content.description: Phase 6: startClaudeWorkflowInteractiveWatch + SessionManager workflow tabs + DisplayController workflow status line. Reference SPEC §12, acceptance T11 T12 T13.

content.subject: [P6] Review plan
content.description: plan-reviewer: tab order (journal first then agents), status line fallback when snapshot null, SessionHandler wiring without double-registration.

content.subject: [P6] Write failing tests (RED)
content.description: Tests for: status line renders workflow name/phase/agent count from snapshot, fallback '(loading snapshot...)' when snapshot null (T13), SessionManager has correct tab labels for workflow journal + agents.

content.subject: [P6] Implement to pass tests (GREEN)
content.description: Add startClaudeWorkflowInteractiveWatch in src/index.ts. Extend DisplayController status line rendering for workflow mode. Wire SessionManager addSession/markSessionDone into WorkflowAttachment via SessionHandler.

content.subject: [P6] Verify and commit
content.description: bun test + typecheck + lint. /commit.

content.subject: [P7] Draft implementation plan
content.description: Phase 7: PaneManager pinAgent/unpinAgent/openPaneEvictIfNeeded + insertionOrder FIFO + --workflow-pane CLI + agent-pick workflow list integration (6-column fzf format) + integration tests + README + CLAUDE.md update. Reference SPEC §11.4, §13.2, §13.3, acceptance T14 T15 T15b.

content.subject: [P7] Review plan
content.description: plan-reviewer: pin set + insertionOrder array invariants on close/evict, --workflow-pane + -i mutual exclusion, agent-pick 6-column format backward compat (col 1+2 hidden).

content.subject: [P7] Write failing tests (RED)
content.description: Tests for: PaneManager.openPaneEvictIfNeeded skips pinned agents during FIFO eviction, pinned agent count != all panes throws/skips, --workflow-pane + -i exits 1 (T15b), agent-pick fzf format includes hidden col 6 for runId, integration test for full workflow run.

content.subject: [P7] Implement to pass tests (GREEN)
content.description: Modify src/terminal/pane-manager.ts (pinAgent + insertionOrder + openPaneEvictIfNeeded). Add --workflow-pane CLI option. Modify src/pick/{fzf-helpers.ts,arg-passthrough.ts,index.ts} for 6-col format + workflow runId routing. Add integration test fixtures.

content.subject: [P7] Verify and commit
content.description: bun test (all) + typecheck + lint. /commit.

content.subject: Run E2E regression
content.description: Run project's e2e script: bun test (full suite). Per SPEC §17.1: tests/claude-workflow/, tests/agents/claude/workflow-agent.test.ts, tests/cli/, tests/integration/. Fix any regression failures before proceeding.
